people always ask me what the hardest part of free solo climbing is and they expect me to say the exposure or the height but honestly it s the weeks before when you re just in a hotel room eating bad pasta and running the route in your head over and over until you re not sure what s real memory and what s imagined

people always ask me what the hardest part of writing a song is and i never have a clean answer sometimes the verse writes itself in the shower and then i sit at the piano for three weeks unable to figure out how to end it that s not mystical that s just annoying
people always want to talk about the dunks  but honestly the play i ve spent the most time on is probably the pick and roll read  that half second decision is where games are actually won
people ask how i stay motivated  honestly  i just really  deeply hate losing to myself  past me set a record and present me finds that offensive
people ask if i still get nervous before a show  i m 85  the only thing i get nervous about is whether the drum stool is at the right height

people ask me what i eat before a big match and honestly it s embarrassing it s usually whatever s left in the hotel minibar that isn t chocolate i had a bag of pretzels and some kind of nut mix before wimbledon one year and played some of the best tennis of my life so now i m sort of afraid to change it

people ask me what i listen to before a match and i always disappoint them it s not some big pump up playlist honestly lately it s been a podcast about the roman aqueduct system i find it genuinely calming

people ask me what i listen to before a match and they always want it to be something intense like heavy metal or whatever it s usually just a podcast about birds i find it genuinely calming my dad thinks that s the saddest thing he s ever heard

people ask me what i m thinking about before a big serve and the honest answer is   nothing  that s the whole job  you spend years filling your head with technique so that when it actually matters  there s nothing left to think

people ask me what it felt like to walk on the moon and i never quite know what to say  because the honest answer is that what i kept thinking about was the checklist  you train so hard that the wonder kind of sneaks up on you later  usually at two in the morning about six months after you re home
people ask me what it s like being in the ocean at four in the morning before a race  honestly  it s the most honest place i ve ever been  the water doesn t care who you are

people ask me what it was like playing opposite brando and honestly the truest answer is confusing you d be in the middle of a scene and he d just stop not forget the line just stop and look at you like you were the one doing something wrong and somehow it worked every time

people ask me what the hardest part of directing is and honestly it s the lunch you ve got forty people with dietary restrictions you re behind schedule the light is dying and someone needs to find a gluten free option for the lead that s the real drama
